parts.text: {"answer":"src/auth/queue.ts deduplicates refresh work per tenant so duplicate refreshes for the same tenant coalesce, while refreshes for different tenants can continue in parallel. A single global mutex was rejected because it would make unrelated tenants block each other, contrary to auth_refresh_different_tenants_parallel.","evidence":{"session_id":"ses_1e1cae151ffeznUA98zPJFnSyO","blob_id":"auth_refresh_queue","message_id":"msg_e1e351ebe001SIQwfHoXmdi78V"},"rationale":"The cited message explicitly states the rejected global-mutex design, why it was rejected, and the intended per-tenant behavior.","irrelevant_context_ignored":["Billing retry queue rationale","Markdown parser onboarding cleanup"]}
